title: Restore demo3 side control transforms and reflect shuffle/A-B toggle state
summary: 0.6.45 fixes the demo3 side control regression by preserving the center control local transform data when cloning to the side screens, and it makes the shuffle and A-B loop toggles reflect live checked state through Trigger.value.
changes: Side-screen control clones now preserve the full center control local transform data before being reparented.
The demo3 builder now emits Trigger.value for both shuffle and A-B loop toggles so runtime can drive their checked state.
Runtime now updates the shuffle and A-B loop toggle values alongside the existing slider and text readback.
reasoning: 0.6.44 ingested the authored center controls correctly but was still too aggressive about rewriting side control transforms, which reset the side stacks back toward the middle basis.
The existing demo readback only updated slider values and text fields, so the toggle surfaces were still cosmetic even though the underlying player state was already known.
operatorVisibleOutcomes: Side control stacks should again preserve the intended layout under the angled screens.
When A and B define and enable a loop, the A-B toggle should show checked.
Shuffle should now show its checked state instead of only changing the label text.
knownIssues: This slice does not yet add interactive A-B toggle enable/disable behavior; it only reflects current state.
The generated scene JSON remains larger than a VaM-saved scene because the generator still writes more verbose JSON.
